learning_outcomes.description: Explain accounting concepts, principles and methods.
Record accounting transactions in accordance with the requirements in International Accounting Standards and Australian Accounting Standards.
Describe the regulatory framework within which International Accounting Standards and Australian Accounting Standards are developed and issued.
Consider corporate governance and ethical issues and implications for the business entity.

assessments.task_description:
Weekly Lecture Revision and Tutorial Preparation will be evaluated via ten quizzes (through Blackboard using RiPPLE). Questions will be multiple choice or short answer and are based on the topic of the week. Each quiz is designed to be closely related to the topic of the week so that you can get prepared for the tutorial and also participate in the tutorial proactively.

Each quiz accounts for 2%. If you miss one quiz, the score for the missed quiz will be zero. No makeup will be available.

You are required to complete the quizzes individually.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: No late submission is allowed. The quiz will be closed automatically past the due time.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment is based on a case of a retailing business.

Assignment materials will be released one week before the due date at 3pm, Friday 4th September.

Topic assessed: Topics 1-4.

Question types: Recording transactions, calculations and short answers.

In preparing for the case study, you should review all lecture and tutorial materials, read all the assigned reading and attempt the practice questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The release and submission of this assessment is via Turn-it-In
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will cover all topics with the main focus on Topics 5 to 10.

The final exam is a paper-based, closed book, invigilated exam by UQ-appointed invigilators.

The final exam is timed to 2 hours with additional 10 minutes of reading time. Please use the 2 hours to answer the questions only. 

The final exam consists of multiple choice questions and written questions including both practical and theoretical components. Information regarding more details of the exam will be provided in the revision lecture.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
